document.title: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions (June 14, 2023)
Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices (May 11, 2005)

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for GT1717C and GT1717CP Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector software and firmware.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software documentation, verification, validation, hazard analysis, traceability, and unresolved anomaly reporting for regulatory technical documentation.
keyPoints: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between GT1717C and GT1717CP.
The GT1717C and GT1717CP software and firmware are the same; the product difference is internal CsI deposition thickness.
The report concludes a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software.
The software consists of SDK functions and onboard firmware/FPGA modules for image acquisition, correction, preprocessing, communication, and firmware update.
Module verification and system validation test tables show pass results, and unresolved anomalies are listed as None.

entities.name: GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions”

entities.name: 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls
entities.evidence: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

entities.name: SDK
entities.evidence: SDK (Software Development Kit): A collection of functions that acquire detector images and perform various manipulations.

entities.name: Onboard Module
entities.evidence: Onboard Module (Firmware/FPGA): This program, embedded in the detector, communicates with the PC console and acquires images from the detector's internal FPGA connected to the panel

concepts.name: Software Level of Concern
concepts.evidence: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software.

concepts.name: Unresolved Anomalies
concepts.evidence: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

concepts.name: Calibration
concepts.evidence: Calibration performs following compensations to acquired images.

concepts.name: Firmware Update
concepts.evidence: The Firmware update module provides a function to read the firmware information of the detector hardware or write the latest firmware

evidence.claim: The document is a Software Validation Report for the GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for GT Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The product models covered are GT1717C and GT1717CP.
The report number and revision shown on the cover are A-SVR-CYN (Rev.1).
The cover approval date is March 23, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Song, Jinsun March 23, 2026; Reviewed R&D Team Manager Lee Kyung Soo March 23, 2026; Approval Quality Manager Hur, Namkyu March 23, 2026

evidence.claim: GT1717C and GT1717CP differ by internal CsI deposition thickness, while software and firmware are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The difference between the GT1717C and GT1717CP products is the internal CsI deposition thickness, but the software and firmware are the same.

evidence.claim: The report concludes the AXD software has a Moderate level of concern.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the AXD software. Level of Concern: Moderate

evidence.claim: The level-of-concern section cites FDA software submission guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: The following section includes the information required in the FDA guidance document entitled “Content of Premarket Submissions for Device Software Functions” (June 14, 2023)

evidence.claim: The development process states it fulfills 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: The detector software is divided into SDK and Onboard Module categories.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector software can be broadly divided into two categories. 1. SDK (Software Development Kit) ... 2. Onboard Module (Firmware/FPGA)

evidence.claim: The SDK includes image correction and preprocessing functions for display of patient images on a PC.
evidence.evidenceText: This is ultimately compiled into a dynamic link library (DLL) and includes various functions (image correction, preprocessing) that enable the final display of patient images on a PC

evidence.claim: The onboard firmware/FPGA communicates with the PC console, acquires detector images, reconstructs them, and transmits them.
evidence.evidenceText: This program, embedded in the detector, communicates with the PC console and acquires images from the detector's internal FPGA connected to the panel, reconstructs them into a two-dimensional pixel array, and transmits them.

evidence.claim: Hardware system requirements include Windows 10 or higher, i5 8th generation or higher, and 8GB DDR4 or higher.
evidence.evidenceText: OS: Windows 10 or higher CPU: i5 8th generation or higher RAM: 8GB DDR4 or higher

evidence.claim: The software development environment uses Microsoft Visual Professional 2022 and C#.
evidence.evidenceText: Program Language Microsoft Visual Professional 2022, C#

evidence.claim: Calibration includes offset correction, gain correction, and defect correction.
evidence.evidenceText: Calibration performs following compensations to acquired images. Offset Correction Gain Correction Defect Correction

evidence.claim: Module verification was evaluated as successful on a standard PC.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of module verification, we positively evaluate the success in installed Software on standard PC.

evidence.claim: System testing found no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies list states None.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

evidence.claim: Hazard controls include CRC and validation checks for communication packets.
evidence.evidenceText: Communication error occurred during the detector is activating. ... Every communication packet must pass CRC and validation checks

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity hazards include unauthorized access and unencrypted data.
evidence.evidenceText: Unauthorized access by unauthorized personnel Cybersecurity ... Unauthorized user can access the unencrypted data Cybersecurity

reviewFlags.reason: Revision evidence is conflicting: sourcePath filename says Rev.2, cover says Report No.: A-SVR-CYN (Rev.1), and the revision record includes dates through April 14, 2026.
Authority is ambiguous: source metadata signals MFDS/domestic, but the source text explicitly cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30, with no MFDS citation in the extracted text.
Manufacturer or legal entity name is not clearly identified in the provided text; AXD appears in several sections but is not defined as the manufacturer.
Some traceability rows appear inconsistent or low quality, including SV2-17, SV2-18, SV2-20, and SRS-6000 mapped to SDS-7000/SV1-7000 while earlier sections use SDS-6000/SV1-6000.
